design_philosophy.core_loop: Select wrestler, engage in one-on-one or tag team matches using standardized moveset of punches, kicks, grapples, and turnbuckle attacks until opponent is defeated
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Positional combat in enclosed or open ring space; steel cage variant adds the constraint of cage escape or knockout requirement
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embodying a WWF professional wrestler competing through multiple match formats to become WWF Champion or Tag Team Champion
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Democratic moveset design where all wrestlers share identical attack options (no unique finishing moves or character differentiation in mechanics), emphasizing accessible competitive play over character specialization; novelty of console-based steel cage match feature

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Roster selection provides some strategic variation; wrestler choice influences championship mode progression difficulty through series of battles

progression.unlock_mechanism: Match-based progression in Championship modes (WWF Championship and Tag Team Championship) requiring defeat of all roster opponents in sequence
replayability_hooks: Multiple match format variations: One-on-One, Steel Cage, Tag Team, Championship modes
10 playable wrestlers per platform version with different rosters across NES vs Sega versions
Tag Team Championship requires cooperative play and different opponent combinations
Multiplayer competitive modes for two-player engagement

world_building_techniques: Licensed WWF roster creates implicit narrative context through real-world wrestling personalities and rivalries
Steel cage match setting provides thematic variation distinct from standard ring matches

faction_indicators: WWF wrestler roster divided across NES version (Hogan, Hart, Roberts, Piper, Justice, Mountie focus) versus Sega version (Flair, Michaels, Tatanka, Papa Shango focus) creates platform-specific allegiances
danger_signals: Steel cage match format represents escalation in match severity/danger within wrestling narrative
environmental_storytelling: Ring and steel cage environments establish wrestling authenticity
Wrestler selection screens implicitly convey personality through roster presentation

sources_used: wikipedia: WWF WrestleMania: Steel Cage Challenge
